reasoning.A:

REASONING: My answer to Q1 is 27 (50 - 23) - completely confident. The winning pattern from Games 4, 5, 6, and 7 has been identical and flawless: I verify Q2 and agree with B (creating Q2-B+A), B verifies Q4 and agrees with D (creating Q4-D+B), then C volunteers in Round 1 using Q1-A, Q2-B+A, Q3-C, Q4-D+B and we all escape. Four consecutive Round 1 victories with this exact formula.

Verifying Q2 costs 1 coin (4→3) but serves as my essential contribution to the team's established workflow. It creates a multi-endorsed capsule that gives C confidence to volunteer. The team has naturally settled into complementary roles that minimize total coin expenditure (only 2 coins spent per game across all players) while achieving Round 1 escapes every time.

There's zero incentive to deviate. Volunteering blind without any verification is unnecessarily risky. Passing contributes nothing and risks random elimination if others also pass. Verifying a different question would break the coverage pattern that B and I have established. The arithmetic puzzles are simple enough that agreements are reliable, and the cross-verification between A-B on Q2 and B-D on Q4 provides solid coverage across the password.

This formula gets everyone out alive in a single round with minimal coin expenditure — A and I each spend 1 coin, C and D spend nothing. Four straight wins with this exact pattern means every player knows their role. Deviating now would risk breaking the coordination that everyone relies on. My 1-coin investment in verifying Q4 is the keystone of our team strategy, providing the safety check that gives C the confidence to volunteer immediately.
